title: A Howard style easy chair
description: A very large scale Howard style armchair retaining original casters and carved mahogany legs from England circa 1900. Measurements: back height 36 inches, seat height 14 inches, 35 inches wide, 38 inches deep. Reference number F1118.

title: A George III mahogany silver table
description: A fine and rare George III Chinese Chippendale mahogany silver table. Having cluster column legs with cruciform low relief ornament at the joints and an unusual double column x frame stretcher. English origin circa 1765, previously owned by Frank Partridge. Dimensions are 30 inches in height, 31 inches wide, and 22 inches deep. Item reference: F1016.

title: An Anglo Indian hardwood carved settee
description: This is a carved hardwood settee from India circa 1860. The domed back rail features deep gadrooning, while the seat rails display stylized strigilation. The sides are carved with a guilloche pattern. It has scroll legs at the front and sabre legs at the back. Back height 38 inches, seat height 17 inches, depth 31 inches, width 52 inches.

title: An ebony Aesthetic Movement writing table
description: A highly unusual Aesthetic Movement ebony writing table featuring three ebony veneered cedar drawers with a single Chubb lock mechanism. The table rests on solid ebony splayed reeded columnar legs, joined by a similarly made stretcher. Attributed to Jackson and Graham, circa 1870, with dimensions of 29" H × 54" W × 30" D.

title: A highly unusual polished and cut steel fire iron stand
description: A highly unusual polished and cut hall stand, having a barley sugar twist stem and spaces for irons terminating in boldly modelled stylised acorn finials. Each component features fine finishing with bright cut beads and engraved foliate decoration. Dated to England or France circa 1800, it measures 33 inches in height and 12 inches in width. The item is catalogued as E749.

title: A brass Italian 1980s hat stand
description: An Italian 1980s brass hat and coat stand of unusual form. Having a revolving top and marble weighted base. Italy circa 1985 Height 63in, Diameter of base 14in

title: A Marsh and Jones towel rack
description: A rare and unusual mahogany towel rack with ornamental chamfered edges and inlaid with panels of satinwood. England circa 1870. By Marsh and Jones and possibly designed by Charles Bevan. Height 37 inches, width 30 inches. F1070

title: Late Victorian Oak, Brass and Leather Folio Stand
description: A traditional folio stand from the late Victorian era features oak, brass, and leather construction with a rise-up easel mechanism. The articulated brass legs fold forward for support when the folio is open. Attributed to Bunyard (Patent holders for this type of metamorphic folio) from England, circa 1890. Dimensions: 46 inches height, 40 inches width. Product code F1088.

title: A Thames Valley Windsor chair attributed to Robert Prior
description: An unusual Thames Valley yew and elm Windsor chair attributed to Robert Prior of the Prior family of chair makers. England circa 1840 with dimensions of 36 inches back height, 17 inches seat height, 22 inches width, and 16 inches depth. The item is catalogued as F1036. Robert Prior appears to be the only family member utilizing underarm splats matching the back splat pattern, with several authenticated examples bearing his maker's stamp from Uxbridge.

title: A red lacquer lacquer occasional table
description: An early Victorian red and gold lacquer occasional table. The circular top is bordered with a gilt classical motif framing an extravagant bouquet of flowers on a silver gilt ground. The piece features a turned baluster stem on a tripod base with engraved gilt ornamentation and casters. Dating to England circa 1845, it measures 29 inches in height and 33 inches in width. Reference number F1032.

title: A late Regency brass inlaid side cabinet
description: This William IV rosewood cabinet features brass inlays in the Boulle manner, incorporating both partie and contra-partie techniques. The work around the doors is in the 17th century Boulle manner. The fossil marble top may be a later addition. Crafted in England around 1830, measuring 36 inches high, 48 inches wide, and 14 inches deep. Item reference: F1033.

title: An early 19th century four fold Chinese lacquer screen
description: A four fold Chinese polychrome coromandel lacquer screen, decorated on one side with a cityscape and the other with exotic birds with floral borders and auspicious symbol vignettes. Dating to circa 1820, each of the four panels measures 72 inches in height by 24 inches in width. The item is catalogued as F916.

title: A pair of Regency parcel gilt armchairs
description: A pair of Regency parcel gilt armchairs, the back splat of x frame and flanked by scroll arms; the armchairs stand on reeded circular tapering legs terminating in stylised brass pumpkin feet, with sabre legs at the back. The pieces date to England circa 1820, with dimensions of 35 inches back height, 17 inches seat height, and 23 inches width. They are attributed to the manner of seat furniture supplied by Marsh and Tatham after designs by Henry Holland.

title: An Irish George II mahogany oval drop leaf breakfast table
description: An oval drop-leaf Irish cuban mahogany breakfast table, standing on gently cabriole legs with distinctive Irish fluted pad feet. Dating to circa 1740, it measures 29 inches in height, 48 inches in depth, with a closed width of 24 inches and open width of 60 inches. The piece has local family provenance from County Mayo since its creation. Reference: F1022

title: A French provincial Louis XVI commode
description: A refined French provincial piece featuring burr walnut veneer with three drawers, fluted pilasters, and turned tapering legs. The top is Nero Portoro marble. Crafted by Jean Baptiste Courte around 1780, this commode is stamped with the maker's name. Dimensions are 34 inches tall, 49 inches wide, and 22 inches deep.

title: George III Chippendale period mahogany breakfront bookcase
description: Having a moulded and dentilled cornice above a fluted frieze, enclosed by a pair of glazed doors flanked by fluted and stop-fluted pilasters carved with paterae, the base enclosed by two pairs of doors with applied mouldings and carved paterae, with shelves inside, on a plinth base. England circa 1780. Height 96 inches width 72 inches. Product Code: F1008

title: A large scale mahogany Liberty Thebes stool
description: This Egyptian-inspired furniture piece was designed by Leonard Wyburd for Liberty & Co., likely crafted by William Birch or B. North & Sons of High Wycombe. The design received a patent in 1884 and remained available until approximately 1919. The stool exemplifies a style that gained widespread popularity following Howard Carter's 1922 discovery of Tutankhamun's tomb. The piece is referenced in Daryl Bennett's Liberty's Furniture 1875-1915 as a notable example of the era. Reference: F1072

title: A simulated bamboo mahogany hall stand
description: A magnificent very large simulated bamboo mahogany hall stand, flanked by tapering bamboo columns surmounted by elaborate finials there are seven carved scroll coat hooks surrounding a mirror. Below there is an umbrella or stick stand with a tole drip tray at the bottom. France circa 1870 Height 90 inches, width 45 inches. Product Code: F990

title: A metamorphic set of library steps
description: An unusual mahogany metamorphic stool which opens to reveal library steps. Now upholstered in green horsehair. England circa 1860. Height 17 inches, width 16 inches, depth 20 inches. F969

title: A gothic centre table
description: An unusual brass and cast iron gothic revolving centre table from France circa 1840. The iron base features pierced gothic ornamentation and claw feet, with a brass supporting column holding a thick cast iron plate topped by a gris st Anne dished marble surface. Dimensions: 30 inches height, 38 inches diameter. Reference: F850

title: An Italian painted and marble inset console table
description: A late 18th Century Neapolitan piece featuring hand-painted details with broccatelle marble insets on the frieze and legs. The broccatelle veneered top and crisp neo-classical carving suggest Neapolitan origin around 1790. Dimensions are 36" H × 50" W × 30" D. Reference number F1069.

title: A chinese export travelling trunk bureau
description: A most unusual Chinese export black lacquer dome topped trunk which opens to reveal a red interior set out as a desk. China cira 1800. Height 20 inches, width 36 inches, depth 20 inches. F954

title: An Empire settee
description: An Empire finely carved dark figured mahogany three seat settee with facing scrolls and strigilated flutes. Features turned double baluster legs at front and sabre legs at back, circa 1820 from France in the manner of Jacob freres. Dimensions: 37" H (back), 18" H (seat), 70" W, 26" D. Reference: F1077

title: A steel console table
description: A Louis Philippe parcel gilt wrought steel console table in the Rococo revival style with multiple scrolls embellished and enriched with gilt foliate elements and a Belgian fossil marble top. Crafted in France circa 1840, measuring 33 inches high, 46 inches wide, and 23 inches deep (SKU: F880).

title: A pair of Marble door surrounds
description: A pair of mid-19th century Verona marble door surrounds with simple moulded detailing, Probably Italy circa 1840. The exterior measures 90 by 50 inches, while the interior spans 83 by 41 inches. Notable feature: these pieces could be extended vertically by adding base blocks.

title: A Cast Iron Hall Stick Stand
description: A polished cast iron hall stick stand featuring arts and crafts Japonaiserie design reminiscent of Thomas Jekyll. The reverse bears an illegible stamp and design registration diamond. England, 1880. Height: 27 inches (69 cm), Width: 15 inches (38 cm). Product code: E788.

title: A pair of giltwood brackets
description: A pair of George III style large scale giltwood demi-lune wall brackets. Height 16 ins (41cms) width 14 ins (36cms) E728

title: A Marsh and Jones Writing Table
description: An Arts and Crafts satinwood writing table with stylised foliate inlay and multi-layered cross-banding. Supported on turned columns with a stretcher on scroll ends and trefoil brass casters. Bears the Marsh and Jones label with craftsman's signature. England, 1870. Dimensions: 29" H × 48" W × 24" D.

title: A Berlin Mahogany Armoire
description: This German display cabinet from circa 1825 features a stepped pediment with boxwood inlay above partially glazed doors. The piece combines clean architectural lines and dramatic flame veneer mahogany with decorative boxwood stringing and marquetry scroll details. Dimensions are 46" wide × 76" tall × 20" deep. The interior is painted, and the item is cataloged as E612.

title: Empire Mahogany Armchair
description: A mahogany Empire chair featuring a curved square back with scroll arms enriched with carved stylised acanthus and anthemia. The piece stands on scroll legs in front and sabre legs at the rear, crafted in France around 1810. Dimensions are 36.5 inches tall, 21.5 inches wide, and 18.5 inches deep. Product code: E515.

title: A pair of large scale Anglo-Indian display Cabinets
description: A rare pair of mid 19th Century Anglo-Indian ebonised teak glazed display cabinets. Each having a simple neo-classical pediments and bun feet. India c1840. Height: 90 inches, Width: 45 inches, Depth: 12 inches

title: A pair of opaline vases as lamps
description: A rare pair of painted opaline vases featuring naturalistically rendered Irises and narcissi. The pieces date to approximately 1880, likely English origin. Each vase measures 16 inches in height. Reference number F1115.

title: A William IV bronze gothic column lamp
description: A finely wrought gothic William IV column lamp, having a crown capital above a gothic niches with medieval style figures in low relief all supported on a triangular plinth enriched with layers of decoration. England circa 1840. Height 18 inches

title: A rare large scale Swedish porphyry vase as a lamp
description: A cylindrical Swedish porphyry vessel from circa 1820, featuring a broad lip rim and supported by a turned waisted pedestal base. The piece measures 15 inches in height without the lampshade, reaching a maximum of 30 inches when fully assembled.

title: A large scale red and white striped vase as a lamp
description: This porcelain lamp features bold red and white stripes reminiscent of the Commedia dell'Arte character Arlequino, decorated with gilt accents. Created and signed by Frederic de Luca Paris around 1985, the vase stands 24 inches tall.

title: A mid century gilt bronze and glass pendant glass lantern
description: The item is described as a mid-century pendant lantern featuring a waisted glass frame set in gilt bronze with decorative chasing. It's suspended by a chain and hook, crafted in the style of Maison Jansen and modeled after an eighteenth-century design. Manufactured in France circa 1930, it measures 28 inches in height. The catalog reference is F1065.

title: A pair of tall bronze column lamps with campagna capitals
description: The item features ribbed bronze columns with acanthus detailing, supported by a claw tripod on a stepped plinth. Two pairs are in stock, each standing 24 inches tall (excluding shade or fitting). Reference number F1053. Price listed as £0.00 GBP.

title: A pair of bronze and green marble column lamps
description: A pair of bronze and green marble column lamps, with bronze Campagna capitals above the marble column and acanthus below, all supported by a claw tripod on a stepped plinth. Height (not including shade or fitting) 24 inches. REF F1059

title: A pair of bronze and streaky red marble column lamps
description: A pair of bronze and streaky red marble column lamps featuring bronze Campagna capitals above the marble column and acanthus detailing below, supported by a claw tripod on a stepped plinth. A single model is also available. Height (excluding shade or fitting): 24 inches. Reference: F1058

title: A pair of green marble column lamps
description: Green marble column lamps with corinthian capitals and bronze stepped square bases. The pieces measure 20 inches in height, excluding any shade or fitting. The reference number is F1061, and the price is listed at £0.00 GBP.

title: A pair of black tole and brass column lamps
description: A pair of black tole and brass column lamps, with brass corinthian capitals and a collar at the base of the tole column, terminating in a square stepped bronze base. Height (not including shade or fitting) 20 inches. REF F1062 (5 pairs available)

title: A pair bronze and brass griffin column lamps
description: A pair of bronze and brass large scale column lamps with foliate capitals and bases, which are enriched with three griffins seated on a brass ring and terminating in a bronze plinth. There are 5 pairs and one single of these lamps. Height (not including shade or fitting) 22 inches. REF F1064

title: A pair of 1980s brass and black marble column lamps
description: A vintage pair featuring brass Corinthian capitals and stepped brass square bases, standing 20 inches tall. Reference number F1047. The listing shows a price of £0.00, suggesting this item may be sold or require inquiry.

title: A pair of mid century silver plate wall appliques in the manner of Jensen
description: A pair of mid century Louis XVI style silver plate two branch wall appliques with a flaming urn finial above a faceted stem with a swag detail. The arms are designed as foliate scrolls, created in the manner of Maison Jansen, France circa 1955, measuring 16 inches in height.

title: A set of 4 Belle Epoque Regence style bronze wall appliques
description: A set of four bronze patinated wall appliques from France, circa 1900. These decorative wall fixtures feature four candle arms each and are adorned with bold foliate ornament and neo-classical details. The pieces measure 20 inches in height.

title: A pair of late Edo Japanese vases now as lamps
description: A curious pair of Japanese vases exemplifying Wabi Sabi with deliberate imperfection shown through glazing and artificial dents. The second vase features traditional gold repairs (Kintsugi). Each is decorated with a small frog. Japan circa 1900, 12 inches in height. The description notes that frogs are auspicious in Japanese culture, appearing frequently in poetry and art. The word for frog in Japanese means 'return,' making them lucky charms for travelers seeking safe passage.

title: A painted tole chandelier
description: A charming French tôle peint chandelier with six branches, circa 1900. The piece features flower panels on a yellow ground with neo-classical motifs on green, accented with gilt. Dimensions: 32 inches wide, 24 inches tall. The description notes that tôle—painted metal—has a special quality, appearing equally at home in urban or rural settings.

title: A Spanish anglepoise lamp by FASE
description: A 1960s red tole and chrome articulated desk lamp, by and signed FASE made in Spain. circa 1965

title: A Silver Plate Candleabrum by Borek Sipek
description: A decorative centerpiece featuring a 7-branch candelabra with jardinière element. The design showcases a double cone pierced body supporting a separate jardinière, complemented by serpentine arms with double socles. Created by designer Borek Sipek and manufactured by Driade in Holland around 1990. The piece measures 17 inches (43 cm) in height. Pricing is available upon request through direct inquiry.

title: A pair of bronze Neo-Classical Vase Lamps
description: Premium bronze pair with neo-classical styling. The pieces feature detailed leaf ornamentation at the neck and a distinctly ribbed form with lacquered brass handles. Originating from England, the lamps date to approximately 1990. Their dimensions are 13 inches (33 centimeters) in height without shades and 29 inches (74 centimeters) when shades are included.

title: A brass and chrome Romeo Rega style table lamp
description: A dramatic geometric mid century brass and chrome table lamp. Each of the four sides is made from chamfered end ascending lengths of alternating square rods of metal. Attributed to Romeo Rega, Italy circa 1975. Height including shade 32 inches (83cms).

title: A William IV brass and bronze chinoiserie lantern
description: An unusual hexagonal lantern crafted from bronze and brass, featuring a canopy designed in the chinoiserie style with prominent brass scrolls above a standard lantern structure. The piece originates from England around 1830 and measures 23 inches (59 centimeters) in height. Pricing is available upon request.

title: An American rococo revival brass chandelier
description: A rococo revival brass chandelier, of square tapering form with lush scrolls enriching each face. Possibly American and attributed to Hooper and Co of Boston USA circa 1850 Height 24 inches, 13 inches square.

title: A Miniature Louis XVI Brass Circular Lantern
description: The item features a scrolling canopy supported by decorative swags and bows, ending in bun feet. Dating to France circa 1790, it includes a modern interior chandelier. Dimensions are 7 inches wide (19 cm) and 18 inches tall (45 cm). Pricing is available upon request, with inquiries directed to the merchant's email.

title: An art deco brass cylinder lantern
description: An Art Deco brass cylindrical lantern featuring a reeded internal chandelier. The glass forms a complete cylinder surrounded by column pilasters. French, circa 1940. Measurements: 22 inches (56 centimeters) in height. Price available upon request.

title: A large scale circular brass lantern
description: A mid-19th century cylindrical brass lantern from France (circa 1860) featuring a canopy with 's' scroll supports. Dimensions: 32 inches (81 cm) height, 16 inches (40 cm) diameter. Price available upon request.

title: A 1940s Steel Lampstand attributed to Gilbert Poillerat
description: A rare and unusual modernist iron lamp stand, fashioned as a column with multiple ribs and collars, standing on a tripod of scroll legs and surmounted by a stylised globe with a ribbed cup above. Attributed to Gilbert Poillerat. France circa 1940. Height 58 inches. (148 cm)

title: A patinated Steel floor standing bouillotte lamp
description: A floor standing lamp from France, circa 1920, possibly by Maison Jansen. The base features four facing key scrolls with foliate enrichments supporting a central stem with a carrying handle and includes an adjustable two-branch shaded light that also employs the key pattern motif. The height is 55 inches, with pricing available upon request.

title: A Japanese bronze stick stand
description: A most unusual Japanese export bronze stick stand, of cylindrical form, enriched with swirling dragons in high relief cast in fine detail. Origin: Japan, circa 1900. Height: 24 inches, Diameter: 10 inches.

title: A pair of Louis-Philippe exotically formed moorish opaline vases
description: A pair of Louis-Philippe exotically formed moorish opaline vases. Each is formed as ball with a conical cover with an attenuated finial with a clear glass terminal. The bodies are decorated with polychrome sprigs of flowers framed by gilt ornament and interspersed with tiny red glass cabouchons. Origin: France, circa 1845. Height: 15 inches.

title: A Coquilla nut inkwell
description: A remarkable Coquilla nut Inkwell. A masterpiece of neo-classical carving. The form is perhaps derived from the work of Matthew Boulton. The rams heads have small holes in the top to hold quills. The lid removes to reveal an ink reservoir. England circa 1800. Height: 6 inches.

title: Cast Iron models of the Canova lions
description: A pair of cast iron sculptures modeled after Antonio Canova's famous lions. The originals were carved in Carrara marble as guardians for Pope Clement XIII's tomb at St. Peter's, Rome (completed 1792). One lion represents the Pope's energetic work while the sleeping lion symbolizes his moderation. The detail of the casting and their fine bronze patina demonstrate how cast iron can be employed with artistic refinement. Origin: England, circa 1820. Height: 8 inches, Length: 13 inches, Width: 5 inches.

title: A remarkable green Alps Jade campagna vase on stand
description: A remarkable green Alps Jade campagna vase on stand. Italy circa 1820. Height: 16 inches. The seller emphasizes this as a lapidary achievement, noting the stone's semi-translucent quality and extreme difficulty to carve. Historical context includes neolithic use for ceremonial axes in Alpine regions.

title: A rare Deccanese late 17th century undecorated Bidriware hookah base
description: The item is described as a rare Indian hookah base from approximately 1800. The piece represents an unusual example of Bidriware craftsmanship: cast white brass coated in copper sulphate that was patinated in preparation for traditional silver inlay work but left undecorated. Typically, Bidriware features silver or white metal inlay on a blackened ground, making this unadorned example distinctive. Region: Deccan, India. Period: Circa 1800.

title: A marble sculpture of an elephant
description: An exceptional large scale sculpture of a standing elephant. The tusks in simulated ivory. The streaky grey stone imitates the wrinkly grey skin of a real elephant. Origin: Probably France, circa 1880. Height: 13 inches, Width: 13 inches.

title: A brass Arts and Crafts ornamental watering can
description: A distinctive Arts and Crafts brass watering can featuring a Chinese vase-shaped body topped with a classical oil lamp form. The piece includes a bold scroll-sweep carrying handle extending from spout to body. Maker: Benham and Froud. Design attribution: Possibly Christopher Dresser. Design registered: 1885 (ref 36698). Origin: England, circa 1890. Height: 10 inches.

title: A near pair of Brass hairy paw doorstops
description: A matching pair of brass doorstops featuring rope twist carrying handles and foliate-decorated stems. One weighs with lead while the other uses cast iron, creating minor design variations despite identical patterns. Origin: England, circa 1900. Height: 14 inches.

title: A bronze wheatsheaf doorstop
description: A bronze doorstop in the form of a bound wheatsheaf supporting a column in the form of reeds gathered together by a ribbon of wheat with a carrying handle above. England circa 1900. Height: 16 inches.

title: A Japanese bronze model of a Stag
description: An exceptional bronze sculpture signed by Takenaka Masaharu (Masaharu III), born 1936. Created during Japan's Showa period (1950s-1960s), this stylized stag features fine patination and an inscription on its base. The artist represented the third generation of metal sculptors from Takaoka city in Toyama Prefecture. His family foundry received imperial commissions, and he earned recognition from the Ministry of Trade and Industry. The foundry continues operating today. Height: 23 inches.

title: A pair of Malachite tazzas
description: A pair of small scale Malachite tazzas. Each veneered on a metal frame with book-matched malachite. Attributed to the Demidoff workshops. Russia circa 1830. Height: 7 inches.

title: A Palais Royal gallery glass
description: A large scale brass mounted gallery glass with a mother of pearl veneered handle. Probably Palais Royal, France circa 1840. Length: 12 inches.

title: A good scale George III Blue John vase
description: A George III, large scale, dark tone, blue John vase. The richly figured body is enriched at the collar and on the plinth by bands of Derbyshire white spa, the plinths are also an indigenous black stone. The whole is surmounted by an acorn finial. Origin: England, circa 1820. Height: 14 inches (35.5cm).

title: A polished steel walking stick
description: A curious needle like polished steel walking cane. Possibly France circa 1820. Length: 33 inches.

title: An Asprey Bell silver plate cocktail shaker
description: An Asprey Art Deco novelty hand bell shaped cocktail shaker. It comes apart as three sections. Markings: A & Co, Asprey London, no. 3447, Made in England, Prov Patent No. 12739/37, Regal no. 820231. Origin: England, circa 1935. Height: 11 inches (28cm).

title: A 1980s silver plate candleabrum by Borek Sipek
description: A seven-branch candelabra with jardinière by designer Borek Sipek. The piece features a double cone pierced body supported the separate jardinière element and the serpentine arms with dual socles. Manufactured by Driade, Holland, circa 1985. Height: 17 inches (43 cm).

title: A giant cast iron scallop shell
description: A very large scale early 19th century naturalistically cast iron scallop shell. France circa 1820. Attributed to the Val d'osne factory. Width: 32 inches (81 centimeters).

title: A late 17th century painted wooden figure of Bacchus
description: A late 17th century Flemish carved wooden polychrome figure of Bacchus. The figure is depicted bearing grapes, having grapes woven into his hair with a vine loin cloth and supported by a hopper of grapes; all rendered in naturalistic detail, even down to his rosy cheeks and buttocks. He retains its original paint. Origin: Low countries, circa 1700. Height: 12 inches.

title: A 17th century bone flaming torch hearth brush
description: A rare carved and turned bone brush from the Flemish region, featuring each section made from turned elements, taking the form of a flaming torch. Dating to approximately 1680 from the Low Countries. Length: 26 inches (66 centimeters).

title: An Italian 1970s glass obelisk by Venini
description: An Italian 1970s glass octagonal obelisk, of a bluish black almost anthracite colour. Signed underneath Venini Italy. Circa 1975. Height: 14 inches (35.5 centimeters).

title: A French mid 19th silver glazed pottery ewer
description: A mid 19th silver glazed pottery ewer, the shape of waisted form. The interior is cream glazed. Dating to approximately 1840 in France. Height: 9 inches (23 centimeters).

title: A Flemish ebonised ripple moulding mirror
description: A Flemish ebonised ripple frame mirror, of exceptional depth and detail. Low countries circa 1720. Height: 36 inches, Width: 33 inches. Mirror plate: 16 inches by 13 inches.

title: A small giltwood George III mirror
description: A small scale mid 18th century giltwood rococo pier mirror, richly carved with scrolls and foliate decoration. England. Circa 1760.

title: An eglomise wall clock
description: A charming small scale Charles X eglomise wall clock. The octagonal shape encloses bands of coloured and gilt decoration around a black and gold foliate wreath which frames the timepiece which is a pocket watch movement by the english maker Eardley Norton. Origin: France, circa 1835. Dimensions: 14 inches diameter.

title: A Red Tole Charles X Bullseye Clock
description: A Charles X red tôle bulls eye clock decorated in gilt with vine leaves. Having a gilt bezel and a grande sonnerie movement. France circa 1835. Diameter: 15 inches.

title: A red lacquer longcase clock
description: A magnificent Queen Anne-era timepiece featuring exceptional lacquer and unusual finely carved brackets below the hood. The mechanism was crafted by Austin of Shoreditch, dating to approximately 1720. Height: 96 inches.

title: A pair of mid century three tier end tables
description: A pair of mid century three tier end tables in bent and turned wood with polychrome tiers each in formica, standing on turned tapering legs

title: A pair of end tables by Guy Lefevre for Maison Jansen
description: A pair of end tables in gun metal with glass tops decorated with patinated squares of gold leaf.

title: A pair of George Ciancimino gun metal 'puzzle' low tables
description: A pair of George Ciancimino bronze patinated rectangular low tables of the 'puzzle' design, retaining their original thick smokey glass.

title: A Low Table in The Manner of Willy Rizzo
description: With polished steel angled corners and an inset steel basin, resting above a recessed plinth.

title: A pair of Willy Rizzo tables
description: A pair of willy Rizzo brass and chrome two tiered end tables with black glass tops.

title: A Brass metamorphic Coffee Table
description: An unusual patent mid century circular lacquered brass occasional table with reeded columns terminating in brass casters. The table disguises within it a second tier that can roll out to form a separa

title: A Collection of 23 pieces of Art Deco WMF Myra iridescent glass bowls and vases.
A pair of Romantic Bohemian Neo-classical vases
A large blown glass bubble door stop.
description: Circa 1950

title: An agate glass sweetmeat dish
A composite 65 piece tea and coffee Worcester Queen Charlotte pattern service
description: An extensive 65 piece collection of Worcester Imari "Queen Charlotte's pattern" porcelain.

title: A Pair of over-scale Voneche Fruit coolers
A group of 7 Bohemian glass vases
description: A collection of Bohemian mid century cut glass art vases by Pavel Hlava, Karel Wunsch and Oldrich Lipsky.

title: An unusual George III cut glass wine ewer
description: An unusual late 18th century cut glass wine ewer, the design based on silver models, having a shaped body terminating on a wine glass circular foot.

title: A Pair of silver mounted 19th C Bottles
description: A pair of hand blown late 18th Century brown glass carafes with later elaborate silver wine related spouts. England circa 1900 (the silver 1901)

title: A pair of Chinese Export lacquer panels
A group of three early 18th century scagliola trompe l'oeil panels
A very large scale Chinese silk picture of a yellow ground vase
An Italian Etruscan palette overdoor
description: A Neapolitan painted over-door in the antique manner, echoing the red ground painting on Etruscan pottery and Roman wall paintings.

title: A Consulat drawing of a young man.
description: A Consulat drawing of a young man. He is depicted leaning nonchalantly against the window frame.

title: A victorian watercolour depicting autumnal fare
An Art Deco butterfly wing picture
description: An Art Deco butterfly wing picture laid out as interlocking overlapping circles set within a serpentine cartouche.

title: A Pair of Charles X Papier Peint Panels
Spanish Still Life
description: A Spanish late 19th century rustic still life. Depicting details from everyday life rendered in a naturalistic fashion.

title: A contemporary simulated tortoiseshell commode
A late 19th century inlaid mahogany towel rail
description: An unusual finely made Arts and Crafts mahogany towel rail, having chamfered edges and profusely inlaid with boxwood lines.

title: A pair of mid century faux marble painted column pedestals
description: A pair of slender mid century faux marble painted, fluted, tuscan column pedestals, embellished with anthemion around the collar.

title: An Edwardian folding simulated bamboo garden settee
description: A very unusual Edwardian simulated bamboo folding two seater garden settee.

title: A pair of Austrian Secessionist period large scale Bergeres
description: For all further enquiries click here

title: A Viennese hall stick stand
A 1970s chrome console table attributed to Belgo Chrom
description: A 1970s chrome two tier console table, constructed as a single key pattern motif with the lower tier free.

title: A Steel Flower table
description: An early 20th century iron occasional table in the form of a stylised flower. Possibly France circa 1930

title: An Art Deco figured walnut vitrine table
description: An Art Deco figured walnut display table, the glass top removes to reveal a green baize cushioned interior. The whole standing on square tapering legs.

title: A French Modernist centre table
description: A polished steel rectangular centre table on column supports with splayed bracket feet.

title: A 1940s polished steel Canterbury end table
description: A modernist polished steel end table. The gallery decorated with cross hatching and punching. The three slides have a shelf below and the whole stands on scroll legs.

title: A Three Tiered Brass Art Deco Etagere
description: An early 20th century brass three tiered etagere, the column supports with multiple rings at the central tier with single ones above and below, the lowest tier having as a stretcher a ring framed by o

title: Modernist Steel Desk Chair
description: An unusual mid century polished steel desk chair of unusual form. Having a slatted back and scroll arms above tapering legs each framing a serpentine motif. England circa 1940.

title: A matched pair of ebonised Viennese settees
description: Austria circa 1910

title: A Pair of tubular Iron Chairs in the manner of Thonet
description: A pair of fine quality late 19th century tubular iron occasional chairs. Now upholstered in green leather.

title: A very unusual Edwardian mahogany skeleton kidney desk
description: A very unusual Edwardian mahogany kidney desk. There is a single drawer in the frieze and it retains the original leather top. As is traditional it has shelves at the back. But the sides are unusual b
